metadata.description: Reusable principles for safe initial evaluation and management of orthopaedic trauma.
Structured identification and treatment of immediate life threats before limb-focused care.
Head-to-toe evaluation after immediate life threats have been addressed.
Coordination of fracture care with physiologic stabilization and resuscitation.
Documented motor, sensory, pulse, perfusion, and vascular assessment distal to an injury.
Repeated neurovascular examinations used to detect interval deterioration.
Arterial or venous injury associated with musculoskeletal trauma.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Traumatic Peripheral Nerve Injury

metadata.description: Peripheral nerve dysfunction caused by trauma, traction, compression, or laceration.
A fracture communicating with the external environment through a traumatic wound.
Classification system for open fractures based on wound, contamination, and soft-tissue injury.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Open Fracture Antibiotic Prophylaxis

metadata.description: Prompt antimicrobial prophylaxis selected for the open-fracture injury pattern and contamination context.
Assessment and treatment of tetanus immunization needs after traumatic wounds.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Open Fracture Irrigation and Debridement

metadata.description: Operative removal of contamination and devitalized tissue with irrigation of an open fracture wound.
Infection occurring in the setting of a fracture and its treatment.
Pathologic elevation of compartment pressure threatening tissue perfusion and viability.
Serial clinical assessment for pain, passive-stretch pain, swelling, tension, and neurologic change.
Adjunctive invasive pressure measurement when the clinical examination is unreliable or equivocal.
Urgent surgical release of involved osteofascial compartments.
Manipulative restoration of alignment without surgical exposure of the fracture or joint.
Controlled force application used during reduction while protecting soft tissues.
Noncircumferential immobilization that maintains alignment while accommodating swelling.
Repeat examination and imaging after reduction and immobilization.
Imaging used to document alignment after reduction and immobilization.
Skin, subcutaneous tissue, fascia, muscle, and neurovascular structures surrounding an injury.
Protection, reassessment, and staged treatment of injured soft tissues.
Temporary stabilization strategy used when physiology or soft tissues make definitive fixation unsafe.
Percutaneous pin-and-frame stabilization used as a temporary trauma construct.
Definitive fixation strategy selected after adequate physiologic response to resuscitation.
Mechanical environment governing displacement and tissue strain at a fracture site.
Interfragmentary compression strategy minimizing motion at a simple fracture.
Construct strategy permitting controlled micromotion and callus formation.
Relative deformation across a fracture gap under load.
Direct bone healing under conditions of anatomic reduction and high stability.
Indirect healing through callus under relative stability.
Fracture healing that progresses more slowly than expected.
Failure of a fracture to progress to union without further intervention.
Fracture healing in unacceptable alignment.
Interfragmentary compression achieved by screw technique or design.
Internal fixation using a plate-and-screw construct.
Load-sharing internal fixation placed within the medullary canal.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Early Postoperative Trauma Care

metadata.description: Immediate postoperative surveillance, mobilization, prophylaxis, and restriction planning after trauma surgery.
Documented limb examination after trauma surgery.
Deep venous thrombosis or pulmonary embolism associated with injury and recovery.
Failure to maintain achieved fracture or joint alignment.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Immediate life threats are addressed before definitive management of an extremity injury.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Document motor, sensory, pulse, and perfusion findings before and after reduction, splinting, or surgery.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: A single normal neurovascular examination does not replace serial assessment when deterioration remains possible.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: A wound near a fracture should be evaluated for communication with the fracture rather than dismissed by wound size.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Open-fracture antibiotic prophylaxis is a time-sensitive component of initial management.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Traumatic wound care includes assessment of tetanus immunization and prophylaxis needs.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Preserved pulses do not exclude acute compartment syndrome.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Compartment pressure measurement is an adjunct when the examination is unreliable or equivocal and should not delay treatment of a clear clinical syndrome.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Urgent reduction of a gross deformity is followed by repeat neurovascular examination and documentation.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Initial splinting should maintain reduction while accommodating expected swelling and protecting skin.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Post-reduction assessment includes alignment imaging and repeat skin and neurovascular examination.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Definitive fixation timing is governed by both patient physiology and the condition of the soft-tissue envelope.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Damage control orthopaedics uses temporary stabilization when physiologic or soft-tissue conditions make definitive fixation unsafe.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Absolute stability is paired with minimal interfragmentary motion and primary bone healing.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Relative stability permits controlled motion and commonly produces secondary bone healing with callus.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Interfragmentary strain links fracture-gap mechanics to the tissue that can form during healing.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Evaluation of impaired union considers mechanical stability, biology, infection, host factors, and time course.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Early postoperative trauma care includes neurovascular surveillance, wound and compartment assessment, mobilization restrictions, and prophylaxis planning.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Hemodynamic instability or unresolved life threat
metadata.action: Prioritize resuscitation and life-saving intervention before definitive fracture fixation

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Absent pulse, impaired perfusion, expanding hematoma, or evolving ischemia
metadata.action: Reduce gross deformity when appropriate and escalate immediately for vascular evaluation and limb-saving management

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Fracture with a communicating wound or high suspicion for communication
metadata.action: Cover the wound, document neurovascular status, initiate indicated antibiotics and tetanus assessment, and arrange operative debridement

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Clinical findings concerning for evolving acute compartment syndrome
metadata.action: Remove constriction, keep the limb appropriately positioned, perform serial examination, and escalate for emergent fasciotomy

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Unreliable or equivocal examination with ongoing concern for compartment syndrome
metadata.action: Use pressure measurement as an adjunct while maintaining serial assessment and urgent escalation

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Gross deformity, threatened skin, dislocation, or neurovascular compromise
metadata.action: Perform timely controlled reduction with appropriate analgesia or sedation and document post-reduction status

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Reduced or unstable acute injury requiring temporary support
metadata.action: Apply a well-padded splint that maintains alignment and accommodates swelling, then repeat examination

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Physiologic instability, severe soft-tissue injury, or unsafe definitive operative conditions
metadata.action: Choose temporary stabilization and defer definitive fixation until physiology and soft tissues permit

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Simple reconstructible pattern versus comminuted or biologically vulnerable pattern
metadata.action: Select absolute or relative stability principles appropriate to fracture morphology and biology

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Failure of expected clinical or radiographic progression toward union
metadata.action: Evaluate mechanical, biologic, infectious, and host contributors before selecting revision treatment

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: New pain escalation, neurologic change, perfusion change, wound concern, or loss of alignment
metadata.action: Repeat focused examination and imaging as indicated and escalate promptly for a limb- or construct-threatening complication
